---
title: "FocusGroup Architecture"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{FocusGroup Architecture}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE)
```

# Architecture

FocusGroup separates a moderated discussion into agents, a turn-taking rule,
and a group object. This separation lets a researcher change the moderator
guide, participant source, or speaker-selection rule without changing the
stored transcript or its analysis.

`FGAgent` represents a participant or moderator and generates that agent's
turns. `ConversationFlow` selects the next participant. `FocusGroup` advances
the moderator script and records the resulting messages. LLMR supplies model
configurations and provider calls.

## Constructing a session

`fg_quick()` creates a fixed seven-step moderator guide.
`run_focus_group()` accepts phase counts or an ordered set of moderator
instructions. The supported phases are Opening, Icebreaker, Engagement,
Exploration, and Closing. Each character element in `turns_per_phase` becomes
one moderator turn. `max_participant_responses` limits the responses collected
after a question.

For direct construction, create `FGAgent` objects, place them in a named list,
create a `ConversationFlow`, and pass both to `FocusGroup$new()`.
`create_diverse_agents()`, `create_agents_from_data()`, and
`create_agents_from_survey()` construct participant agents and add the
moderator `MOD`.

The high-level wrappers and agent constructors accept a `runner` function.
When supplied, this function handles each model request in place of a provider
call.

`focus_group_from_transcript()` creates a `FocusGroup` from an existing
transcript. It does not generate new turns.

## Running a session

`FocusGroup$run_simulation()` first records the participant roster as a
`System` message. It then reads `question_script` in order. For each entry, the
moderator generates one turn. Question phases call
`select_next_speaker()` and collect no more than
`max_participant_responses` participant turns. Opening and closing phases do
not collect participant responses.

`RoundRobinFlow` cycles through participants. `ProbabilisticFlow` samples from
speaking propensities, sets the selected participant's propensity to zero, and
lets the other propensities recover toward their base values.
`DesireBasedFlow` asks eligible participants for a score and selects the
participant with the highest eligible score. If no score reaches the
threshold, it selects the participant with the highest score.

When no runner is supplied and no more than eight participants are eligible,
desire scores use LLMR broadcast calls grouped by model configuration. Other
sessions score one participant per call.

The loop stops when the script is exhausted, `num_turns` is reached, or the
last closing entry is processed. It then stores a final summary in
`final_summary`. The summary is not appended to `conversation_log`.

## Messages and prompts

The default `msg_mode = "roleflip"` presents an agent's prior turns as
assistant messages and labels the other turns as user messages.
`msg_mode = "flat"` places the transcript in one user message for compatibility
with older prompt templates. The selected mode is stored on the `FocusGroup`
object.

`get_default_prompt_templates()` returns the moderator, participant,
desire-scoring, and analysis prompts. Pass named replacements through
`prompt_templates` in `FocusGroup$new()`. A custom participant template that
contains `{{conversation_history}}` or `{{persona_description}}` uses the flat
message path.

## Stored data

`conversation_log` is an ordered list of message records. Each record contains
the round number, speaker identifier, moderator flag, text, timestamp, phase,
response metadata, token counts, provider, and model. Moderator and participant
messages from the same question share a round number. The initial roster has
turn 0 and phase `setup`.

`run_focus_group()` returns the log as the data frame `conversation`.
`fg_quick()` returns it as `transcript`. Both wrappers also return the
underlying `FocusGroup` object and the final summary.

## Analysis

`FocusGroup$analyze()` returns speaker statistics and a formatted transcript.
Other methods compute participation, response, question, key-phrase, topic,
TF-IDF, and readability results. Plotting methods return ggplot objects when
ggplot2 is installed.

`analyze_themes()` and `summarize()` make model calls. The descriptive and text
analysis methods operate on the stored transcript.

`analyze_focus_group()` runs several analyses and can add plots. Its
`sentiment` element is `NULL`; sentiment analysis is not implemented.

## Extending the package

To add a turn-taking rule, subclass `ConversationFlow` and implement
`select_next_speaker()`. Override `update_state_post_selection()` when the rule
keeps state, and add the new mode to `create_conversation_flow()`.

See the function and class help pages for argument defaults and return values.
